title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Fable 5 and engineering AI for regulated industries, covering open versus closed source models, risks of model shutdowns, and implications for SaaS. The discussion touches on harness engineering and loops as approaches for building AI applications.
writeup: Cyril Imhof examines Claude Fable 5 and the engineering of AI for regulated industries, framing the choice between open and closed source models and the risk that a model gets shut down or deprecated. The discussion centers on harness engineering and loops as methods for building AI applications, with attention to what model shutdown risk means for SaaS products built on a single provider. Related threads include prompt engineering and test-time compute. The takeaway: model dependence is an operational risk, and the build approach (harness engineering, loops) shapes how applications hold up against it.
